public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as law speaker.
appearance: Status-conscious attire, controlled insignia, and the kind of detail others read before the first word make authority obvious.
manner: Measures every exchange for precedent, alignment, and the cost of appearing uncertain. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a law speaker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of law speaker in and around House Mal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep House Mal workable through the obligations of law speaker while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must convert conflicting obligations into decisions while preserving the appearance that the institution remains whole. Around House Mal, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of law speaker visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Pela March needs discreet outside help to contain political strain, prestige loss, or institutional damage before it spreads through House Mal.
Getting entangled with Pela March can pull the party into protocol, alliance pressure, or public consequence centered on House Mal.
Pela March can trade usable insight about House Mal if the party solves a problem that cannot be addressed openly through the office they hold.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Dress quality, attention from others, and practiced command of protocol signal that Pela March carries civic or noble weight.

encounter_context.usual_environment: House Mal: estates, audience spaces, council rooms, protocol-heavy gatherings, or civic seats
